import React, { useState } from 'react'; import SEOHead from '../components/SEOHead'; export default function VoltageDivider() { const [vin, setVin] = useState(''); const [r1, setR1] = useState(''); const [r2, setR2] = useState(''); const [result, setResult] = useState(null); const [error, setError] = useState(''); const calculate = () => { setError(''); const vinN = parseFloat(vin), r1N = parseFloat(r1), r2N = parseFloat(r2); if (isNaN(vinN) || isNaN(r1N) || isNaN(r2N) || r1N <= 0 || r2N <= 0) { setError('Please enter valid positive values.'); return; } const vout = vinN * r2N / (r1N + r2N); setResult({ vout, r1: r1N, r2: r2N, vin: vinN }); }; const clear = () => { setVin(''); setR1(''); setR2(''); setResult(null); setError(''); }; return ( <>

How Voltage Dividers Work

A voltage divider is a simple circuit which turns a large voltage into a smaller voltage using two series resistors.

The Formula

Vout = Vin × (R2 / (R1 + R2))

This assumes no load is connected to the output.

Usage

Commonly used for level shifting, creating reference voltages, or measuring high voltages with a microcontroller.
